title: ampelopsis engelmannii
intro: Ampelopsis engelmannii, also known as Engelmann's grapevine, is a type of flowering plant in the grape family (Vitaceae) native to the southwestern United States. The plant's leaves and berries have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ampelopsis engelmannii include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is used in herbal remedies for its potential health benefits.
